project_type: pnpm monorepo with Turborepo (9 apps + 27 packages), Node.js 22, TypeScript ESM
build_system: pnpm workspace + Turborepo, each package has tsc --noEmit for typecheck
test_framework: Vitest with vi.fn() mocking, no real database needed - tests mock PrismaClient. Root vitest.config.ts with globals: true
build_command: unset NODE_OPTIONS && npx tsc --noEmit -p apps/quantchat/tsconfig.json && npx tsc --noEmit -p apps/quantmail/tsconfig.json && npx tsc --noEmit -p apps/quantai/tsconfig.json
verification_instructions: 1. Run vitest to confirm all tests pass. 2. Run tsc --noEmit on modified apps to confirm TypeScript compiles.
snapshot_or_generated_files: packages/database uses prisma generate for @prisma/client types. Already generated.
setup_instructions: pnpm install already done. Node 22 available. Must unset NODE_OPTIONS before running vitest/tsc.
environment_constraints: COMMON_DEPENDENCIES network - can install npm packages. No PostgreSQL running - tests must mock Prisma. Must unset NODE_OPTIONS env var before running node commands due to proxy bootstrap issue.
contribution_requirements: TypeScript strict mode, ESM modules, use workspace packages (@quant/database, @quant/server-core, @quant/ai, @quant/realtime, @quant/auth). Each app should use Fastify via @quant/server-core createApp pattern.
key_patterns: 1. Repositories extend BaseRepository with PrismaClient injection. 2. Fastify apps use createApp() from @quant/server-core with Zod validation. 3. Tests mock PrismaClient with vi.fn(). 4. AI services accept AIEngine and wrap engine.infer() calls. 5. Real-time uses WebSocketServer/ChannelManager/PresenceManager from @quant/realtime. 6. Auth uses TokenService from @quant/auth with jose JWT. 7. The quantmail api-v2 is the reference pattern for new Fastify-based APIs.
directory_structure: apps/quant{chat,mail,ai,sync,ads,tube,neon,edits,max} - 9 apps. packages/{database,auth,ai,realtime,server-core,common,shared-ui,...} - shared infrastructure. Each app has api/ (old custom router) and some have api-v2/ (new Fastify-based). Phase 6 creates new backend/ dirs with production-grade services.
